report_name: LogiTrans Global Paralyzed by $45M QuantumLock Ransomware Attack, Global Supply Chains Disrupted

threat_actors.name: QuantumLock
threat_actors.description: A newly emerged, sophisticated Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that conducted a devastating attack on LogiTrans Global. The group uses double-extortion tactics, exfiltrating data before deploying ransomware and demanding payment in Monero.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of LogiTrans Global for $45 million.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The attackers gained initial access by compromising a VPN gateway that was not protected by multi-factor authentication.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exploited vulnerability CVE-2026-23456 on a domain controller to escalate privileges and gain administrative control.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated approximately 5 TB of sensitive data, including customer information, contracts, and financial records, before deploying the ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The QuantumLock ransomware was deployed to encrypt critical systems, including large files and database systems, to paralyze LogiTrans Global's worldwide operations.

malware.name: QuantumLock Ransomware
malware.description: An advanced ransomware that exhibits features designed to maximize damage by prioritizing the encryption of large files and database systems. It was used to encrypt critical systems at LogiTrans Global.

tools.name: vssadmin.exe
tools.description: The vssadmin.exe utility was mentioned as a tool likely used by the ransomware to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-23456
vulnerabilities.description: A known critical vulnerability in a domain controller that was exploited by the QuantumLock group to escalate privileges to administrative control.

identities.name: LogiTrans Global
identities.description: A global logistics provider that was the primary victim of the QuantumLock ransomware attack, leading to a worldwide halt in its operations.

infrastructure.name: Compromised VPN Gateway
infrastructure.description: A VPN gateway lacking multi-factor authentication that served as the initial access point for the QuantumLock attackers.

infrastructure.name: QuantumLock Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web leak site where the QuantumLock group threatened to publish the 5 TB of data exfiltrated from LogiTrans Global.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b1a987c-5432-4f1e-8e7d-9c8b6a5f4d3e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/logistics-giant-logitrans-global-paralyzed-by-quantumlock-ransomware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-01
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of `vssadmin.exe` to delete volume shadow copies, a technique commonly used by ransomware like QuantumLock to inhibit system recovery.
